name: Pad Thai Stir-Fried Rice Ribbon Noodles
recipeIngredient: Stephen Ceideburg: 1 tablespoon Small dried shrimp (optional)
6 ounces Dried flat rice noodles (see note)
1/4 pound Medium shrimp, shelled and deveined (optional)
Boneless chicken breast: half, skinned, thinly sliced
Chopped preserved radish: 2 tablespoons soaked in warm water for 10 minutes, drained (optional)
Lime wedges: 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. Place noodles in a bowl and cover with warm water; let soak about 15 minutes or until soft and pliable. Drain and set aside.
2. Set a wok over medium-high heat. When hot, add 2 tablespoons of the oil. Add shrimp and chicken; stir-fry until shrimp turns bright orange and chicken turns white, about 30 seconds. Set aside.
3. Reheat wok over medium heat. Add the remaining oil and garlic; brown gently (about 20 seconds). Increase heat to medium-high. Add bean sauce, tomato paste, fish sauce, vinegar, and sugar; stir until sugar dissolves.
4. Increase heat to high. Break one egg into the sauce; beat three or four times. Let cook until egg is slightly set but still moist, about 20 seconds. Repeat with second and third eggs. There should be tiny flecks of egg peeking through.
5. Add optional radish, dried shrimp, tofu, chili flakes, softened noodles, and a handful of bean sprouts to the hot wok. Toss and lift noodles until tender and no longer stiff, about 3 or 4 minutes.
6. Add chicken broth (2 tablespoons at a time) if noodles seem dry, green onions, and reserved shrimp/chicken mixture; toss for 30 seconds to reheat. The sauce should "dry-coat" the noodles.
7. Transfer to a platter and serve with a sprinkling of chopped peanuts, chili flakes to taste, and fresh coriander leaves. Arrange remaining bean sprouts and lime wedges around noodles (squeeze lime over noodles before eating).
8. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Note: Traditionally, Pad Thai uses dried, flat Y4-inch-wide fettuccine-like rice-flour noodles, labeled "Chantaboon rice sticks."
